static.description: A polyglot microservices platform for food delivery, built with 5 independent services (API Gateway, Order, Inventory, Driver, Notification) across Kotlin, Java, Python, Go, and TypeScript stacks, communicating via RabbitMQ events and fronted by a JWT-authenticated API Gateway.
domain_concepts: OrderStatus: Enum representing the lifecycle states of an order: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED, with CANCELLED and REFUNDED as terminal/side states.
OrderItem: An embeddable JPA entity representing a line item within an order, containing menu item ID, name, quantity, and unit price.
MenuItem: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant menu item with stock_quantity (-1 means unlimited) and availability tracking.
Driver: A MongoDB document representing a delivery driver with name, phone, vehicle type, availability status, and GPS location.
GeoPoint: A struct/document embedded in Driver storing latitude and longitude coordinates for real-time location tracking.
JwtAuthFilter: A Spring Cloud Gateway global filter that validates JWT tokens and forwards the verified customer ID as an X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.
acquire_lock: A Redis-based distributed lock function in the Inventory Service that prevents concurrent stock overselling by acquiring an exclusive lock on a menu item.
order.events: The RabbitMQ exchange name used for publishing order lifecycle events that the Notification Service consumes.
stripePaymentIntentId: The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (pi_xxxx) stored on an Order, used for manual capture and refund operations.
Restaurant: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and associated menu items.
isValidTransition: A static method on OrderStatus that enforces the allowed state machine transitions for order lifecycle.

dynamic.architecture_summary: The platform follows a microservices architecture with an API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) that performs JWT authentication and routes requests to three backend services: Order Service (Java/Spring Boot with PostgreSQL and Stripe payments), Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI with PostgreSQL and Redis distributed locks), and Driver Service (Go with MongoDB). A Notification Service (Node.js/TypeScript) consumes RabbitMQ events to send SMS via Twilio and email via SendGrid. A React frontend (TypeScript/Vite/Tailwind) provides customer, restaurant, driver, and admin views.

key_components.name: API Gateway
key_components.responsibility: Routes all external HTTP requests to internal services and enforces JWT authentication via a global filter.

key_components.name: JWT Auth Filter
key_components.responsibility: Validates JWT tokens on incoming requests and forwards the verified customer ID as an X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.

key_components.name: Order Controller
key_components.responsibility: REST API for creating, listing, retrieving, updating status, and cancelling orders.

key_components.name: Order Service
key_components.responsibility: Business logic for order lifecycle management including payment intent creation, status transitions with validation, cancellation, refunds, and RabbitMQ event publishing.

key_components.name: Order Model
key_components.responsibility: JPA entity representing an order with line items, payment info, delivery address, and timestamps.

key_components.name: OrderStatus
key_components.responsibility: Enum defining order lifecycle states and valid transition rules.

key_components.name: Payment Gateway
key_components.responsibility: Interface abstracting Stripe payment operations (create intent, refund) to allow test mocking.

key_components.name: Stripe Payment Gateway
key_components.responsibility: Live Stripe SDK implementation for creating payment intents with manual capture and issuing refunds.

key_components.name: Inventory Service
key_components.responsibility: FastAPI application handling restaurant listings, menu item queries, and stock reservation with Redis distributed locks.

key_components.name: Inventory Models
key_components.responsibility: SQLAlchemy ORM models for Restaurant and MenuItem with stock quantity tracking.

key_components.name: Driver Service
key_components.responsibility: Go HTTP service for driver CRUD, location updates, and availability management backed by MongoDB.

key_components.name: Notification Service
key_components.responsibility: Consumes order events from RabbitMQ and sends SMS (Twilio) and email (SendGrid) notifications with idempotency protection.

key_components.name: Frontend App
key_components.responsibility: React SPA with role-based views (customer, restaurant, driver, admin) for interacting with the platform via the API Gateway.

key_components.name: Auth Context
key_components.responsibility: React context that manages JWT token storage in localStorage and extracts the customer ID from the token's sub claim.

key_components.name: API Client
key_components.responsibility: Axios instance configured with the gateway base URL and automatic JWT Bearer token injection.

key_components.name: Frontend Types
key_components.responsibility: TypeScript type definitions for Order, Restaurant, MenuItem, Driver, and related DTOs shared across frontend components.

agent_safe_operations: Adding new REST endpoints to existing service controllers/handlers
Adding new unit tests to any service's test directory
Modifying frontend React components and views
Adding new frontend API client functions in frontend/src/api/
Adding new TypeScript types in frontend/src/types/index.ts
Updating inventory-service FastAPI route handlers in main.py
Adding new SQLAlchemy models in inventory-service/models.py
Adding new Go handler methods in driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go
Modifying notification message templates in notification-service/src/index.ts
Updating order status transition rules in OrderStatus.java
Adding new dependencies to requirements.txt, package.json, pom.xml, go.mod, or build.gradle.kts
agent_forbidden_operations: Modifying JWT secret values or authentication bypass logic in JwtAuthFilter.kt
Changing Stripe API key handling or payment gateway security in StripePaymentGateway.java
Removing or weakening order status transition validation in OrderStatus.isValidTransition
Disabling Redis distributed locking in the inventory service stock reservation flow
Modifying the RabbitMQ exchange/queue naming conventions used across services
Changing database migration schemas without coordinating across services that share PostgreSQL
Removing idempotency checks in the notification service
Hardcoding secrets or credentials in source files
Changing the X-Customer-Id header contract between the gateway and downstream services
potential_pitfalls: Each service uses a different language, framework, and build system — changes must use the correct idioms (Java/Spring, Python/FastAPI, Go stdlib, TypeScript/Node).
Order status transitions are strictly validated in OrderStatus.isValidTransition; adding new statuses requires updating the switch expression and all consuming services.
The inventory service uses Redis distributed locks for stock reservation — any stock-modifying code must acquire and release locks correctly or risk deadlocks/overselling.
The Order Service, Inventory Service, and Notification Service communicate via RabbitMQ with specific exchange/routing key conventions (order.events, order.*) — changes must be synchronized.

The API Gateway forwards X-Customer-Id header from JWT claims — downstream services trust this header implicitly, so any changes to the header name or JWT claim structure break auth across all services.
The order-service uses Lombok annotations (@Getter, @Setter, @Builder) — IDEs and build tools must have Lombok configured or compilation will fail.
Python inventory-service uses async SQLAlchemy (asyncpg driver) — all database operations must use async/await patterns, not synchronous SQLAlchemy APIs.
The Go driver-service uses a DriverStore interface for testability — new database operations must be added to both the interface and the mongoDriverStore implementation plus the mockStore in tests.
The frontend dev login view generates JWTs with a hardcoded dev secret that must match the gateway's JWT_SECRET in development — mismatched secrets cause silent 401 errors.
The notification service's in-memory idempotency Set resets on restart — duplicate notifications can occur after service restarts until this is replaced with Redis.
PostgreSQL is shared between order-service and inventory-service — schema changes in one service can affect the other if tables/columns overlap.
The frontend uses @tanstack/react-query with specific query keys — cache invalidation depends on matching these keys exactly when adding mutations.
